resume.raw_text:
=== Leila Titikpina - Resume.pdf ===
Leila Titikpina  
347-573-7603 | leilatitikpina92@gmail.com  

EDUCATION  

Ithaca College | Ithaca, NY                                                                                                            Graduated May 2026   
 Bachelor of Science in Health Science  

EXPERIENCE  

Level 4 CNYMRC | Ithaca, NY                                                                                                      Fall 2025  
Volunteer  

•  Managed the Flu POD on campus with 520 students and staff vaccinated  
•  Recorded the length of the vaccination for people from the entrance to the exit  
•  Provided information to the mental health screening and guidance to the provider for further inquiry  

Platinum Home Care | Brooklyn, New York  
Home Health Aide  

July 2023 – April 2025  

●  Completed 75 hours of training focused on assisting patients at home, emphasizing professionalism in the 

relationship between patients and home health aides.  

●  Provided valuable support to patients in Activities of Daily Living, including grocery shopping and house 

cleaning, ensuring a comfortable and well-maintained living environment.  

●  Played a crucial role in patient care by reminding individuals of medication schedules and upcoming doctor 

office visits, contributing to their overall well-being.  

Woodhull Community Care (NYC Health+ Hospital)                                                          July 2024 – Aug.2024 
Case Management Intern                                               

•  Created a condensed training for case management positions such as how to use Epic, and VMware to assess 

patient eligibility for Home Health Aide in the Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, and Queens.  

•  Helped with the inventory of community care by classifying shirts and tote bags into a giveaway for the 

community.  

PrIMES mentorship                                                                                                             Jan.2024-Aug.2024  
(mentee) 
Completed weekly meetings with my mentor and spoke about each week's module in preparation for medical school 
admission  

•  Attended different meetings tailed on mentee learning about medical school decision-making and meet 

different professionals in the medical field.  

North Manhattan Eye Center | New York, NY  
Intern and Physician Shadowing  

June 2023 – Aug.2023  

●  Guided patients to examination rooms, engaging with them to understand worries and background information, 

gaining valuable insights into their perspectives.  

●  Participated in brainstorming sessions centered around improving the work environment, gaining foresight into 

expectations and challenges as a future physician.  

 
  
  
  
 ● 

Interacted with physicians and staff, learning about equipment roles and the diagnostic process, enhancing  
knowledge on how diagnoses are made.  

●  Strengthened observational and listening skills by actively participating in patient cases, identifying patterns 

and recognizing unusual circumstances that may lead to the diagnosis of eye pathology.  

College Access: Research & Action |Bronx, NYC  

July 2021 – June 2022  

●  Participated in extensive training focused on college and career access, acquiring valuable insights and 

knowledge to facilitate students’ access to post-secondary opportunities.  

●  Provided one-on-one assistance and advisory group support to peers, aiding in their college and financial aid 

applications.  

●  Collaborated with staff to support the planning and execution of college and career events, contributing to the 

overall success of these initiatives.  

RESEARCH  

Microbiology Research Project | Ithaca, NY  

September 2023 – May 2024  

● 

In collaboration with Professor Kari Brossard, studied the antimicrobial resistance in the bacterial DNA of 
monkey feces and the possible implications on human health and antibiotic development.  

●  Acquired proficiency in pipetting and utilizing a microcentrifuge for DNA experiments, conducted PCR and 
DNA extraction, ran gel electrophoresis for PCR, and demonstrated expertise in counting bacteria colonies in 
petri dishes.  

ACTIVITIES  

●  IC Muslim Student Association / President (September 2024- May 2026)  
●  IC Muslim Student Association | Treasurer (September 2023 – May 2024)  
●  Ellis Preparatory Academy Professional Workshop Series | Participant (April 2021 – June 2021)  
●  BioBus | Participant (February 2021 – May 2021)  
●  Speakhire | Peer Leader ( November 2020 – June 2021)  
●  St. John the Divine Soup Kitchen | Volunteer (Thanksgiving 2020)  

SKILLS  

●  Languages: English (Advanced), French (Fluent), Tchamba (Native), Ewe (Native)  
●  Technology: Microsoft word (Proficient), Powerpoint (Proficient), Adobe Acrobat (Proficient)  

Certification  

•  Adult and Pediatric First Aid/CPR/AED                                                                               October 2025  

Awards  
 Cum Laude                                                                                                                           May 2026 
29th Annual James J.Whalen Academic Symposium                                                            April 2026 
27th Annual James J.Whalen Academic Symposium                                                             April 2024  
Academic excellence                                                                                                            April 2023-2025  
Student Staff                                                                                                                         April 2025  
Academic Honors                                                                                                                  April 2025  
Alpha Alpha Alpha Honor Society                                                                                         April 2025

experience.title: Volunteer
experience.description: Managed the Flu POD on campus with 520 students and staff vaccinated. Recorded vaccination timings from entrance to exit. Provided information for mental health screenings and guided individuals to providers.

experience.title: Home Health Aide
experience.description: Completed 75 hours of professional training. Assisted patients with Activities of Daily Living including grocery shopping and house cleaning. Reminded patients of medication schedules and upcoming doctor visits.

experience.title: Case Management Intern
experience.description: Created condensed training materials for case management positions covering Epic and VMware. Assessed patient eligibility for Home Health Aide services across the Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, and Queens. Assisted with community care inventory for community giveaways.

experience.title: Mentee
experience.description: Completed weekly meetings with a mentor covering modules in preparation for medical school admission. Attended meetings on medical school decision-making and met professionals across the medical field.

experience.title: Intern & Physician Shadowing
experience.description: Guided patients to examination rooms and gathered background information. Participated in brainstorming sessions on improving the work environment. Interacted with physicians and staff to learn diagnostic processes and eye pathology identification.

experience.title: College Access Advisor
experience.description: Trained in college and career access. Provided one-on-one assistance to peers on college and financial aid applications. Collaborated with staff to plan and execute college and career events.
